title: Aspect preview
subtitle: Imperfective vs perfective — what you already know, applied to verbs

vocabulary.exampleMeaning: I was reading the book for an hour.
He was speaking for a long time.

hook.text: You already understand the concept of imperfective and perfective from Unit 9's placement — this is an early application of that knowledge. Every Russian verb exists as a pair: imperfective (process, ongoing, habitual) and perfective (completed, one-time result). Today you see these pairs in practice with the verbs you've already learned.

why.text: Aspect is not optional in Russian — every time you choose a past or future tense verb, you're choosing a form. Getting it wrong doesn't make a sentence ungrammatical, but it changes the meaning significantly. Since you already grasp the concept, today's goal is recognising the pairs, not production. Production comes in Unit 9.

cards.title: The aspect pair — one concept, two verbs
cards.body: Every action in Russian has two verb forms. Imperfective: describes the process, duration, repetition, or ongoing state. Perfective: describes a completed event with a result. Think of imperfective as 'watching the film' and perfective as 'the film is over'.

cards.title: Present tense — imperfective only
cards.body: Present tense in Russian can only be imperfective. Why? Because a completed action can't be happening right now — by definition it's already done. So all the present tense conjugations you've learned so far are imperfective. This matters when you add perfective verbs to your vocabulary.

examples.meaning: I am reading. / I read. — ongoing, present
I was reading / I used to read.

cards.title: How perfective pairs are formed
cards.body: Most perfective verbs are formed by adding a prefix to the imperfective. The most common prefixes: с- (сделать), про- (прочитать), на- (написать). The prefix doesn't change the stem — only the aspect. Some pairs are completely different words (говорить/сказать) — these must be memorised.

questions.prompt: You were reading for two hours but didn't finish. Which verb?
questions.explanation: Two hours of reading = process without specified completion → imperfective. Я читал книгу два часа.

questions.prompt: You finished reading the book. Which verb?
questions.explanation: Finished = completed result → perfective. Я прочитал книгу.

questions.prompt: Which tense can ONLY use imperfective verbs?
questions.explanation: Present tense = imperfective only. A completed action can't be happening right now.

cards.title: Aspect in practice — the key contexts
cards.body: Three signals that tell you imperfective is needed: (1) 'was doing / used to do' in past — process; (2) 'doing right now' — present tense; (3) question 'what were you doing?' — focus on process. Three signals for perfective: (1) 'did and finished'; (2) 'will do (once, result)'; (3) sequence of completed events.

examples.meaning: He spoke for a long time (process).
He told the truth (one statement, completed).

cards.title: Unit 9 preview
cards.body: You already know the conceptual distinction between imperfective and perfective — that's confirmed by your placement score. This lesson is about recognising pairs in context. Full production — forming future tense with both aspects, and navigating complex aspect choices — is the focus of Unit 9. Don't try to master it now; notice it.

examples.meaning: I will be reading. / I will read (ongoing).

questions.prompt: Which is the perfective of делать?
questions.explanation: делать (impf) / сделать (pf). The perfective uses the prefix с-.

questions.prompt: Я ___ книгу (I finished reading the book). Which verb?
questions.explanation: прочитал = read and finished. Completed result → perfective.

options: Two verbs with the same meaning
A suppletive aspect pair — different roots
The same verb in two tenses
questions.answer: A suppletive aspect pair — different roots
questions.explanation: говорить (impf, ongoing speech) / сказать (pf, one utterance). Different roots — must be memorised.

questions.prompt: Which verb do you use in present tense?
questions.explanation: Present tense is always imperfective — a completed action can't be happening now.
сделать = to do / complete (perfective of делать). The с- prefix marks completion.
прочитать = to read through and complete (perfective of читать).

questions.prompt: He was speaking for an hour — which aspect?
questions.explanation: 'For an hour' = duration = process = imperfective. Он говорил час.

rules: Imperfective: process, duration, repetition, ongoing state. Perfective: completed event with result.
Present tense is always imperfective — completion and the present moment are contradictory
Most perfective verbs: prefix + imperfective stem (с-, про-, на-, etc.)
Some aspect pairs are suppletive (different roots): говорить / сказать
Full aspect mastery in Unit 9 — for now, recognise the pairs
summary.nextLesson: Next: Unit 5 — the case system. All 6 cases, starting with an overview and the two you'll use most: nominative and accusative.

questions.prompt: Which aspect describes a completed action?
What is the perfective of читать?
Я ___ книгу (I finished the book). Which form?
говорить / сказать is an example of:
'He was speaking for a long time' uses which aspect?
